title: ARPA-H Awards $22M for Anti-Retrotransposon Aging Trial
description: A multi-institutional consortium led by University of Rochester will test TPN-101, an antiviral drug, to suppress retrotransposon-driven inflammation in 200 adults aged 60-65, funded by ARPA-H's PROSPR program.

title: First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial Cleared by the FDA
description: Life Biosciences received IND clearance for ER-100, the first-ever partial epigenetic reprogramming trial in humans, targeting glaucoma and NAION with results expected by year-end.

title: Klothea Bio Launches Phase 1b Alpha-Klotho mRNA Trial
description: Klothea Bio began a randomized, double-blind Phase 1b study of AKL003, an alpha-Klotho mRNA therapeutic, enrolling 21 healthy adults to measure longevity biomarkers and safety.

title: Longeveron Stem Cell Therapy for Age-Related Frailty Published in Cell Stem Cell
description: Phase 2b results demonstrate allogeneic stem cell therapy improved condition of patients with age-related frailty, published in the high-impact journal Cell Stem Cell.

title: $80M NIH Grant Renews Long Life Family Study for Longevity Genetics
description: The NIH renewed the Long Life Family Study with $80M to analyze 7,800 participants using long-read sequencing to identify genetic variants that protect against aging.

title: Scientists Turn Gut Bacteria into Anti-Aging Factories
description: Howard Hughes researchers found low-dose cephaloridine prompts gut bacteria to produce colanic acid, extending worm lifespan and improving cholesterol and insulin markers in mice without systemic side effects.

title: Rapamycin: Comprehensive Review Shows Limited Human Evidence Despite Animal Promise
description: A 2026 Aging Cell review and multiple meta-analyses confirm rapamycin is safe for ~1 year in healthy adults but insufficient evidence exists to confirm longevity benefits in humans.

title: Longevity News Roundup: Nanoplastics, Klotho mRNA Trials, and FDA Regulatory Shift
description: Weekly roundup covers nanoplastics crossing the blood-brain barrier, Klothea mRNA human trials, wearable lactate sensors, and the FDA ending the two-trial dogma for drug approvals.

title: Life Biosciences Secures First FDA Approval for Longevity Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, leads the longevity race with FDA-approved Phase 1 trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming for vision restoration, ahead of Altos Labs and NewLimit.

title: Longevity Biotech Stocks Surge as $27 Trillion Healthcare Shift Accelerates
description: Cell therapy markets are projected at $8.85B by 2026 and AI drug discovery at $24.51B, as institutional capital rotates into regenerative medicine and longevity infrastructure.

title: Multi-Scale Longevity: New Preprint from Michael Levin on Defeating Aging
description: Tufts scientist Michael Levin published a Springer Nature chapter on multi-scale longevity, framing aging as a challenge to embodied minds and proposing collective cell intelligence approaches.

title: Dr. Rhonda Patrick: Weight Training Reduces Brain Age by 1.4-2.3 Years
description: Rhonda Patrick highlighted research showing one year of strength training reduced older adults' brain age by up to 2.3 years with effects persisting at two-year follow-up.

title: Rhonda Patrick on Heat Exposure as Antidepressant and Longevity Tool
description: A single session raising core body temperature produced an antidepressant effect lasting 6-8 weeks; combined with CBT showed approximately 16-point improvement on depression scales.

title: GLP-1 Pills, Gene Therapy in Dogs, and Amazon Longevity Integration
description: Oral GLP-1 drugs advance from Novo and Lilly; GenFlow's SIRT6 gene therapy shows improved survival in aged dogs; Amazon One Medical integrates LifeForce longevity biomarkers.

title: The 10 Breakthroughs That Changed Longevity in 2025
description: Comprehensive review of 2025's top longevity advances: sleep as top predictor, AI screening 1.1B compounds, senolytic RCTs, organ-specific aging clocks, combination therapies, and partial reprogramming in animals.

title: Top 10 Longevity Breakthroughs of 2025: PEARL Trial, GLP-1, SGLT2 Inhibitors
description: Key 2025 milestones include the PEARL rapamycin trial in women, GLP-1 drugs as potential first longevity medications, SGLT2 inhibitors lengthening telomeres, and urolithin A cardioprotection.

title: Could Humans Live to 150? Researchers Debate Lifespan Extension
description: Nature explores the longevity debate: IL-11 blockers extended mouse lifespan 25%, rapamycin-calorie restriction meta-analysis, and sirtuin-NAD+ clinical trials showing reduced inflammation markers.

title: Fight Aging Newsletter: CAR-T for Brain Plaques, Partial Reprogramming of Memory Neurons
description: Weekly digest covers CAR-T therapy targeting amyloid plaques, partial reprogramming improving cognitive function in aged mice, IL-6 and cognitive impairment, and cytomegalovirus in neurodegeneration.

title: Senolytics Review: From Pharmacological Inhibitors to Immunotherapies
description: Comprehensive review of senolytic approaches including dasatinib+quercetin, navitoclax, fisetin, antibody-drug conjugates targeting B2M and ApoD, CAR-T cells, and the GPNMB vaccine extending lifespan in mice.

title: The Longevity Show 2026 Confirms First Partners
description: The 2026 Longevity Show at Tobacco Dock, London announces partners spanning biotech, diagnostics, AI, nutrition, and investment including ProLon, Viome, Technogym, and Quris.ai.

title: The Ethical Case for Treating Aging as a Medical Condition
description: Analysis argues the moral burden now falls on defenders of forced aging to justify preventable suffering, grounding longevity research in autonomy, self-ownership, and intrinsic value of life.

title: Mitochondrial Circular RNAs Decline with Age, Link to Cellular Senescence
description: New research shows circMT-RNR2 and GRSF1 regulate the TCA cycle and cellular senescence, suggesting mitochondrial RNA targeting could slow aging.

title: Telomere Science and Age Reversal: Research Compilation Goes Viral on X
description: A viral thread compiling telomere science research including HBOT telomere extension in older adults garnered 117K impressions and 700+ bookmarks, showing growing public interest in age reversal.

title: PEARL Trial: Low-Dose Weekly Rapamycin Shows Lean Mass Benefits in Women
description: The landmark PEARL trial demonstrated 48 weeks of low-dose intermittent rapamycin is safe in healthy adults, with significant lean tissue mass and pain improvements in women at the 10mg dose.

title: Gimv Pulls Back from Early-Stage Aging Biotech, Signals Capital Shift
description: European investment firm Gimv reduces early-stage aging biotech exposure, redirecting capital toward diagnostics, digital longevity platforms, and tech-enabled services with faster ROI.
